# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

// Connect to the database
$dsn = 'mysql:host=localhost;dbname=your_database';
$username = 'your_username';
$password = 'your_password';
try {
    $pdo = new PDO($dsn, $username, $password);
} catch (PDOException $e) {
    echo 'Connection failed: ' . $e->getMessage();
}

// Handle the $_POST data
$status = $_POST['status'] ?? null;
$category = $_POST['category'] ?? null;
$startDate = $_POST['start_date'] ?? null;
$endDate = $_POST['end_date'] ?? null;

// Build the SQL query based on the user-selected filters
$sql = 'SELECT * FROM reports';
if ($status) {
    $sql .= ' WHERE status = :status';
}
if ($category) {
    $sql .= ' AND category = :category';
}
if ($startDate && $endDate) {
    $sql .= ' AND date BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date';
}

// Prepare the SQL query with parameterized placeholders
$stmt = $pdo->prepare($sql);

// Bind the parameters to the prepared statement
if ($status) {
    $stmt->bindValue(':status', $status, PDO::PARAM_STR);
}
if ($category) {
    $stmt->bindValue(':category', $category, PDO::PARAM_STR);
}
if ($startDate && $endDate) {
    $stmt->bindValue(':start_date', $startDate, PDO::PARAM_STR);
    $stmt->bindValue(':end_date', $endDate, PDO::PARAM_STR);
}

// Execute the SQL query and fetch the results
$stmt->execute();
$reports = $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);

// Display the reports in a table or other format
echo '<table>';
foreach ($reports as $report) {
    echo '<tr><td>' . $report['id'] . '</td><td>' . $report['status'] . '</td><td>' . $report['category'] . '</td><td>' . $report['date'] . '</td></tr>';
}
echo '</table>';

// Close the database connection
$pdo = null;
